name: Birds in the Nest
recipeInstructions:
These cookies have been a "must" on my family's holiday table since I was a little girl. My earliest memories of "birds in the nest" take me back to my maternal grandmother's house where she filled each "nest" according to the favorite jelly flavor of each of her grandchildren.
1. In a large bowl, cream together the sugar and butter or shortening until smooth.
2. Add egg yolks, water, flour, vanilla, and salt. Mix well until a dough forms.
3. Form the dough into a ball and place it in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to firm up.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
5. Remove the dough from the refrigerator and break off small walnut-sized pieces.
6. Dip each piece first in beaten egg whites, then roll in ground nuts.
7. Flatten each piece slightly and press a finger into the center to make a small well.
8. Fill the well with a drop of jelly.
9. Place the cookies on a greased baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
10. Bake for 15-20 minutes or until lightly golden.
11. Remove from oven and cool completely on wire racks before serving.
Yield: about 4 dozen
